data.explanation: Parenthetical phrase “with its crew” does not change singular subject “The ship.”
data.statement: The ship, with its crew, was lost.

easy.question: Pick the singular verb
The horse and carriage {is} at the door.

data.explanation: Singular subject “the girl” takes singular verb “walks.”

easy.question: Which verb is correct? “The girl ___ every morning.”

data.explanation: “Bread and butter” considered one item → singular verb “is.”

easy.question: Choose the correct verb: “Bread and butter ___ his favourite breakfast.”

data.explanation: Compound subject joined by “and” → plural verb.
data.statement: He and I were playing.

easy.question: Pick the plural verb.

data.explanation: Two people joined by “and” take a plural verb.

easy.question: Which is correct? “He and I ___ at school.”
Neither of the boys {was} guilty.

data.explanation: Collective noun “committee” treated as a single unit → singular verb.

easy.question: Which verb fits? “The committee ___ its report.”

data.explanation: “Each” as subject requires singular verb.
data.statement: Each of these is expensive.

easy.question: Pick the correct singular verb.
Every boy and every girl {was} given a prize.

data.explanation: “Being praised” is a gerund phrase serving as object of “of.”
data.statement: She is tired of being praised.

easy.question: Pick the gerund acting as object.

data.explanation: When subjects joined by or/nor, verb agrees with nearer subject (“dog” → singular “has”).

easy.question: Correct sentence: “Either the cat or the dog ___ been fed.”
None of the students {were} absent.

data.explanation: “Every … and …” requires singular verb (“wins”).

easy.question: Which verb is correct? “Every boy and girl ___ prizes.”

data.explanation: “Director” nearer verb → singular.
data.statement: Either the managers or the director is arriving.

easy.question: Pick the verb that agrees with nearer subject.
The average worker’s earnings {have} gone up.

data.explanation: “News” is singular in English → “is.”

easy.question: Choose the right verb: “The news ___ surprising.”

data.explanation: “Means” (income) takes plural verb.
data.statement: His means are ample.

easy.question: Pick plural verb for “means” (income sense).
The rise and fall of the tide {is} due to lunar influence.

data.explanation: “Neither” followed by singular noun → singular verb.

easy.question: Which is correct? “Neither of the answers ___ acceptable.”

data.explanation: Verb is singular because construction requires singular agreement.
data.statement: Neither praise nor blame seems to affect him.

easy.question: Pick the singular verb with “neither/nor.”
My means {were} much reduced.

data.explanation: “Mathematics” singular in meaning → singular verb.
data.statement: Mathematics is a branch of study.

easy.question: Pick the phrase that is treated singular.

data.explanation: “Pair” is singular collective → singular verb.

easy.question: Pick correct form: “The pair of shoes ___ on the shelf.”
Great pains {have} been taken.

data.explanation: “Pains” can take plural when meaning many efforts.
data.statement: Great pains have been taken.

easy.question: Pick the plural verb.

data.explanation: Plural measure considered as a single distance → singular verb.

easy.question: Choose correct verb: “Ten kilometres ___ a long distance.”
Silver, as well as cotton, {has} fallen in price.

data.explanation: Committee as a whole → singular verb.
data.statement: The committee has issued its report.

easy.question: Pick the singular verb for collective considered as one.
The committee {is} divided on the matter.

hard.question: When the plural noun is a proper name for an object, it {must} be followed by a singular verb.
The committee, when thought of as individuals, {are} divided.

data.explanation: Verb agrees with nearer subject (“students”) → “have”; second blank expects plural “are” in continuation — combined to test nearer-subject rule.

hard.question: Choose best verb: “Neither the teacher nor the students ___ been informed, but the nearer plural subject suggests ___.”

data.explanation: “Mathematics” singular in meaning.
data.statement: Mathematics is difficult for many.

hard.question: Pick the phrase that makes subject singular despite plural form.

data.explanation: Parenthetical phrase doesn’t change the main subject.
data.statement: The ship, with its crew, was lost at sea.

hard.question: Pick the words showing parenthetical phrase not affecting verb.
None, though properly singular, commonly {takes} a plural verb in informal usage.

data.explanation: “Directors” nearer → plural verb.
data.statement: Neither the chairman nor the directors are in favour.

hard.question: Pick correct nearer-subject agreement.

data.explanation: “Means” plural here → plural verb.

hard.question: Which is correct? “All possible means ___ been tried.”
According to present market rate twelve dozen {cost} one hundred rupees.

data.explanation: Quantity considered as a whole → singular verb “is.”

hard.question: Select correct: “Three parts of the business ___ left for me to do."

data.explanation: Both singular; verb singular.

hard.question: Which option is best? “Neither the price nor the quality ___ been satisfactory.” (price singular, quality singular)

data.explanation: Committee viewed as members → plural verb.
data.statement: The committee are arguing among themselves.

hard.question: Pick the verb for collective noun treated as individuals.
The means employed by you {is} sufficient.

data.explanation: Amount as single sum → singular.
data.statement: Fifty thousand rupees is a large sum.

hard.question: Pick the singular verb for quantity considered as whole.

data.explanation: In usage “Bread and butter are served together” (plural form), but when thought as one dish “is regarded as one” — tests context sensitivity.

hard.question: Choose correct verb: “Bread and butter ___ often served together but ___ regarded as one dish.”
Either he or I {am} mistaken.

data.explanation: Verb agrees with nearer subject “he.”
data.statement: Neither you nor he is to blame.

hard.question: Pick the correct verb when or/nor subjects differ in person.

data.explanation: “Either of” takes singular verb.

hard.question: Which is right? “Either of the proposals ___ acceptable.”
One of the students {has} already left.

data.explanation: “Means” plural → plural verb.
data.statement: All possible means have been tried.

hard.question: Pick the correct verb for “pains and means” consistent usage.

data.explanation: “None” with uncountable “evidence” is singular → “is.”

hard.question: Choose correct: “None of the evidence ___ conclusive.”

data.explanation: Proper name for a collective unit → singular.
data.statement: The United States has a big navy.

hard.question: Pick singular verb for collective unit as a whole.
Pains and means must be used {consistently}.

data.explanation: Plural usage of “none” referring to people → plural verb.

hard.question: Which is correct? “None but the brave ___ ever believed it.”

data.explanation: Subjunctive mood for unreal condition.
data.statement: If it were possible to get near, we should see it.

hard.question: Pick the correct subjunction usage (subjunctive).
Neither of the two men {was} very strong.

data.explanation: “None” with plural countable sense commonly plural.
data.statement: None of the pupils are ready.

hard.question: Pick the correct verb for “none” with countable noun.

data.explanation: “Every one” singular → “has.”

hard.question: Choose the correct verb: “Every one of the reports ___ been read.”
When two singular nouns suggest one idea, the verb {is} singular.

data.explanation: When considered as a single dozen, singular verb.

hard.question: Pick correct: “A dozen eggs ___ on the table.” (considered as a unit)

data.explanation: Subject “The introduction” is singular despite following nouns → singular verb.

medium.question: Choose the correct verb: “The introduction of tea and coffee ___ some effect.”

data.explanation: Parenthetical phrase doesn’t change subject.
data.statement: The ship, with its crew, was lost.

medium.question: Pick the singular verb.

data.explanation: Subjunctive “if it were possible” is standard after hypothetical.

medium.question: Which is correct? “If it ___ possible to get near…”
Either the teacher or the students {are} to blame.

data.explanation: Verb agrees with nearer plural subject “employees.”
data.statement: Neither the manager nor the employees are present.

medium.question: Pick the nearer-subject rule.

data.explanation: “Every … and …” requires singular verb.
data.statement: Every boy and every girl was given sweets.

medium.question: Pick the singular verb after “every.”

data.explanation: Measure considered as a single distance → singular verb.

medium.question: Choose proper verb: “Ten miles ___ a long walk.”

data.explanation: “Means” with monetary sense often plural.
data.statement: My means were much reduced.

medium.question: Pick the plural verb for “means” (income sense).
The results of the recognition of this fact {are} seen.

data.explanation: “Neither” takes singular verb.
options: Neither of the answers are correct.
Neither of the answers were correct.
Neither of the answers have been correct.
Neither of the answers is correct.

medium.question: Which sentence is correct?

data.explanation: Title treated as singular work.
data.statement: Gulliver’s Travels was written by Swift.

medium.question: Pick the singular treatment of a plural-titled book.
Neither praise nor blame {seems} to affect him.

data.explanation: “Each” requires singular verb.

medium.question: Correct agreement: “Each of these substances ___ found in India.”

data.explanation: Committee considered as members → plural.
data.statement: The committee are divided on this point.

medium.question: Pick the plural verb for collective individuals.

data.explanation: Parenthetical “as well as” does not make the verb plural; subject “The guidance” singular.

medium.question: Which is best? “The guidance, as well as the love of a mother, ___ wanting.”
Two or more singular nouns joined by and {require} a plural verb.

data.explanation: Verb agrees with nearer subject “I.”
data.statement: Either he or I am mistaken.

medium.question: Pick correct in “either … or” with persons of different persons.

data.explanation: When one subject joined by nor is plural, verb agrees with nearer (directors) → plural.

medium.question: Pick correct: “Neither the chairman nor the directors ___ in favor.”
The United States {has} a big navy.

data.explanation: “Politics” is singular in meaning here → singular verb.

medium.question: Choose correct verb: “Politics ___ for him the business of his life.”

data.explanation: “Each” requires singular verb.
data.statement: Each day and each hour brings its duty.

medium.question: Pick the singular verb after “each.”
If one idea is meant, the verb {is} singular.

data.explanation: If “friend and benefactor” refers to same person, treat as singular → “has.”

medium.question: Which is right? “My friend and benefactor ___ come.”

data.explanation: “Mathematics” singular in meaning.
data.statement: Mathematics is a branch of study.

medium.question: Pick the plural noun with singular meaning (maths).
The means employed by you {is} sufficient.

data.explanation: Amount considered as single sum → singular verb.
data.statement: Fifty thousand rupees is a large sum.

medium.question: Pick the correct verb for amounts.
Cows {are} amongst the gentlest creatures.

data.explanation: “None” commonly takes plural when meaning “no people” → plural verb.

medium.question: Choose correct: “None but fools ___ ever believed it.”
The Arabian Nights {is} still a favorite.
Much pains {has} been taken.
